sections.title: What this project does
sections.summary: The repository implements Hierarchical Nearest Neighbors (HNN), a custom token‑free AI that generates large Q&A datasets with an LLM (see distill.py), embeds the text into 1024‑dimensional vectors, clusters them hierarchically into prototype trees, and answers user queries by traversing this tree, delivering fast, low‑cost retrieval (README.md).
highlights: Generates 50k Q&A pairs via Ollama LLM (distill.py, README.md)
Creates multi‑level prototype tree using KNN and KMeans (README.md)
Provides near‑zero runtime cost query answering (README.md)
Supports multiple backends: Ollama, Gemini, sentence‑transformers (README.md)

sections.title: Technologies & frameworks
sections.summary: The project is built in Python and relies on three main backend technologies: Ollama for local LLM inference, Google Gemini for cloud embeddings and chat, and the Sentence‑Transformers library for local embedding generation. It also uses classic machine‑learning methods like K‑Nearest Neighbors and K‑Means for clustering the vectors.
highlights: Ollama backend for local model serving
Gemini API for cloud embeddings and chat
KNN and K‑Means clustering to build the hierarchical tree

sections.title: Architecture & structure
sections.summary: The repository centers on a single Python script (distill.py) that builds a hierarchical nearest‑neighbor tree: it embeds raw Q&A data, clusters embeddings across three levels, quantizes prototypes, and saves them as ollama_hnn.npz and source_data.csv. Configuration is handled via a .env file, and the README.md provides usage instructions and documentation.
highlights: distill.py contains the full build pipeline (embedding, KNN clustering, prototype creation, saving).
Configuration via .env (example in .example.env) as described in README.md.
Root folder includes README.md, distill.py, requirements.txt, .env/.example.env, and generated data files.

sections.title: Senior engineering signals
sections.summary: The codebase is well‑structured with clear separation of data generation, embedding, and hierarchical NN building, offers flexible multi‑backend support (Ollama, Gemini, local sentence‑transformers) configured via a .env file, includes robust error handling during batch generation, and provides scalable, adjustable HNN construction with simple CLI usage (README.md, distill.py).
highlights: Configuration via .env for easy switching – README.md
Batch generation with try/except error handling – distill.py
Adjustable scale (testing vs production) – README.md & distill.py
Hierarchical NN build with configurable levels/k – distill.py
